title: baeckea gracilis
intro: Baeckea gracilis, also known as the 'Native Rosemary' or 'Pine Bush', is a small, shrub-like plant native to eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it a valuable resource for treating various health issues. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in essential oils, including borneol, bornyl acetate, and camphor, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Baeckea gracilis can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried powder for various herbal applications.
